skill.name: render-automation
skill.description: Automate Render cloud platform operations via MCP: manage services, trigger deployments, monitor status, and organize projects.
skill.summary: Automate Render deployments and service management through Composio's Render toolkit via Rube MCP.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings are false positives. The SKILL.md file is documentation only - backticks are markdown formatting for code examples, not executable Ruby/shell commands. No actual security risks detected.

content.user_title: Automate Render cloud deployments and services
content.value_statement: Manual Render deployments and service management consume valuable DevOps time. This skill automates Render platform operations through MCP tools for services, deployments, and projects.
actual_capabilities: List and browse Render services with filtering by name, type, and pagination
Trigger manual deployments with optional cache clearing
Monitor deployment status and poll for completion
Resolve service names to IDs via API lookups
limitations: Requires active Rube MCP connection with Render toolkit configured
Cannot access services without proper Render authentication
Deployment monitoring requires manual polling at intervals
Rate limits apply to Render API calls

use_cases.title: DevOps Engineer
use_cases.description: Trigger deployments after code reviews and monitor progress without leaving the chat interface

use_cases.title: Full-Stack Developer
use_cases.description: List services by name, check deployment status, and manage projects during development cycles

use_cases.title: Platform Team
use_cases.description: Automate routine Render operations across multiple services and environments

prompt_templates.title: List all Render services
prompt_templates.prompt: List all my Render services and show me which ones are web services versus static sites.

prompt_templates.title: Deploy a specific service
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the service named 'api-production' and trigger a new deployment with cache clearing enabled.

prompt_templates.title: Monitor deployment progress
prompt_templates.prompt: Check the status of deployment 'dep-xxxx' on service 'srv-yyyy' and tell me when it reaches live status.

prompt_templates.title: Complete deployment workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Deploy my 'frontend-prod' service, then poll the deployment status every 20 seconds until it completes. Report any failures with error details.

output_examples.input: List all Render services with type filter for web_service
output_examples.output: Found 3 web services: api-production (srv-abc123), frontend-prod (srv-def456), worker-main (srv-ghi789). All services currently live with recent deployments.

output_examples.input: Trigger deployment for service srv-abc123 with cache clear
output_examples.output: Deployment triggered successfully. Deploy ID: dep-d2mqkf9r. Status: build_in_progress. Started at 2026-02-24T23:15:00Z.

best_practices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to get current tool schemas before any operations
Use 10-30 second intervals when polling deployment status to avoid rate limits
Resolve service names to IDs via RENDER_LIST_SERVICES before trigger operations
anti_patterns: Rapid polling of deployment status without delays causes rate limit errors
Assuming service IDs without verification leads to wrong service deployments
Ignoring deployment terminal states and continuing to poll indefinitely

faq.question: What is required to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need Rube MCP configured with an active Render connection. Call RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS with toolkit 'render' to set up authentication.

faq.question: How do I find my service ID?
faq.answer: Use RENDER_LIST_SERVICES with the name parameter to search for your service. The response includes the service ID in the format 'srv-xxxxxxxxxxxx'.

faq.question: Are deployments synchronous or asynchronous?
faq.answer: Deployments are asynchronous. Use RENDER_TRIGGER_DEPLOY to start, then RENDER_RETRIEVE_DEPLOY to poll status until it reaches 'live' or a failed state.

faq.question: What deployment statuses should I expect?
faq.answer: Statuses include: created, build_in_progress, update_in_progress, live, deactivated, build_failed, update_failed, and canceled. 'Live' indicates success.

faq.question: How does pagination work for listing services?
faq.answer: Use cursor-based pagination. Set limit (max 100), then use the cursor from each response to fetch the next page until no cursor is returned.

faq.question: What happens if a deployment fails?
faq.answer: Failed deployments do not auto-rollback. The previous version remains live. You must manually trigger a new deployment to fix issues.
